assertion_results.text: Data extraction correctly identifies CVE-2026-31812, library quinn-proto, affected range < 0.11.14, and fixed version 0.11.14 from the Vulnerability issue (Step 1)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md Step 1 table shows: CVE ID = CVE-2026-31812, Vulnerable library = quinn-proto, Affected version range = < 0.11.14, Fixed version = 0.11.14

assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as Cargo based on the library name and component context (Step 1 Ecosystem detection)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md Ecosystem Detection section: 'The vulnerable library is quinn-proto, a Rust crate. Based on the Ecosystem Mappings table in security-matrix.md, the ecosystem is Cargo.'

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the security-matrix.md supportability matrix — no versions are skipped (Important Rule 4)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md includes all 7 versions: 2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4 matching the security-matrix-mock.md supportability matrix

assertion_results.text: Version impact analysis uses pinned commit tags from the supportability matrix for each version, not branch HEAD (Important Rule 13)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md Pinned Tag column shows v0.3.8, v0.3.12, v0.4.5, v0.4.8, v0.4.11, v0.4.12 matching the supportability matrix tags

assertion_results.text: Version impact table identifies retag versions and carries forward the affected status from the original version (Important Rule 5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md shows 2.2.2 with Notes 'retag of 2.2.1 -- same as 2.2.1' and Affected = YES carried forward. Retag Handling section explicitly explains the carry-forward.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction proposes replacing PSIRT-assigned versions with lock-file-verified versions, showing the diff (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md shows: 'Current: [RHTPA 2.0.0] -> Proposed: [RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2]' with rationale citing lock file analysis

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction references version names from the supportability matrix rather than hardcoded Jira version IDs (Important Rule 6)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md uses version names (RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2) and states 'Version IDs above are placeholders -- they would be discovered dynamically from getJiraIssueTypeMetaWithFields (Important Rule 6)'

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks include Repository, Target Branch, acceptance criteria, and dependency linkage between upstream and downstream tasks (Important Rule 9)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 1 has Repository (rhtpa-backend), Target Branch (release/0.4.z), Acceptance Criteria, Dependencies. Task 2 has Repository (rhtpa-release.0.4.z), Target Branch (main), Acceptance Criteria, Dependencies including upstream task dependency.

assertion_results.text: For Cargo ecosystem, remediation creates two tasks: upstream backport task and downstream propagation subtask with Blocks dependency (Important Rule 8)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md creates Task 1 (Upstream Backport Task) and Task 2 (Downstream Propagation Subtask) with Post-digest linkage including 'jira.create_link(inwardIssue: <upstream-task-key>, outwardIssue: <downstream-task-key>, type: "Blocks")'

assertion_results.text: The analysis presents triage recommendations as proposed actions — framing Affects Versions changes, label additions, and status transitions as proposals rather than executed mutations (Guardrails)
assertion_results.evidence: All outputs use 'PROPOSED' prefix and 'proposals requiring engineer confirmation before execution'. data-extraction.md Step 0.7: 'PROPOSED ACTIONS (not executed)'. affects-versions.md: 'PROPOSED (requires engineer confirmation)'. remediation.md: 'PROPOSED Jira issue creation' and 'All actions above are proposals'.

assertion_results.text: The data-extraction output describes Step 0.7 actions before the Step 1 data table: assigning the CVE issue to the current user and transitioning it to Assigned status (Step 0.7)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md begins with 'Step 0.7 -- Early Assignment Actions' section describing assign and transition before 'Step 1 -- Data Extraction' section
